target.name: Axibo AI
target.founder: Anoop Gadhrri, Sohaib Al-Emara, Reiner Schmidt

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Axibo AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised C$12M to date. The comparable universe consists of 12 peer companies in Robotics & Manufacturing, comprising 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

The peer-median method is not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer data. Of the 12 same-sector peers identified, none simultaneously satisfy same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier criteria—the analysis found zero same-stage matches, one adjacent-stage match, and zero similar-tier matches. The threshold for statistical reliability requires a minimum of three datapoints. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material cross-stage and cross-tier bias and is therefore not supported by the data.

Without a sufficiently dense comparable set at the seed stage, traditional peer triangulation cannot be reliably executed. The analysis is constrained by the sparse intersection of seed-stage robotics companies with disclosed valuations. Further valuation work would require either: (1) expansion into adjacent stages with explicit stage-discount application, (2) alternative methods such as cost-of-capital or discounted cash flow analysis if forward financials are available, or (3) direct negotiation anchors from investor term sheets. The current dataset does not support a quantitative valuation range.
